title: phoradendron leucarpum subsp. macrophyllum
intro: Phoradendron leucarpum subsp. macrophyllum, also known as the American mistletoe or oak mistletoe, is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of oak trees. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune system function. The herbal preparations of Phoradendron leucarpum subsp. macrophyllum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, syrups, and salves for various health applications.
